title: Define crate error types and validate module scaffold
purpose: Establish the norn crate as a compilable workspace member with the correct module structure, lint configuration, and a comprehensive error type hierarchy. This is the foundation that every subsequent brief depends on — nothing can be built until the crate compiles cleanly with proper error propagation.
task: The crate scaffold already exists (created during reference extraction): Cargo.toml, lib.rs with module declarations, and the full directory tree with empty files. This brief validates that scaffold against C1-C3, then fills in C4 by defining the NornError type hierarchy in error.rs. All error types are defined in error.rs (matching DESIGN.md: 'error.rs -- crate error types (thiserror)'). Each module re-exports its relevant error type via pub use. Testing layer: unit tests (Layer 1) — error types are testable in isolation.

requirements.title: Validate workspace membership and edition
requirements.spec: The norn crate SHALL be a workspace member with edition matching the workspace default. Cargo.toml SHALL declare version.workspace, edition.workspace, and license.workspace.
acceptance: `cargo check -p norn` succeeds with zero errors
Cargo.toml uses version.workspace = true, edition.workspace = true, license.workspace = true
The crate appears in the root Cargo.toml workspace members list

requirements.title: Validate lint configuration
requirements.spec: Cargo.toml SHALL declare `[lints] workspace = true` inheriting the workspace-level unsafe_code = deny and pedantic clippy lints. The crate SHALL NOT define its own lint overrides.
acceptance: `cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings` produces zero errors on the scaffolded crate
Cargo.toml contains `[lints] workspace = true` and no `[lints.clippy]` or `[lints.rust]` sections

requirements.title: Validate lib.rs module declarations
requirements.spec: src/lib.rs SHALL declare public modules matching the DESIGN.md Structure section: provider, loop, tool, tools, rules, agent, session, integration, error. The `loop` module SHALL use raw identifier syntax (`r#loop`). lib.rs SHALL contain only module declarations and crate-level documentation.
acceptance: lib.rs contains exactly 9 `pub mod` declarations: agent, error, integration, r#loop, provider, rules, session, tool, tools
lib.rs contains no struct, enum, fn, impl, trait, const, or static declarations
Every declared module resolves to a directory with a mod.rs file

requirements.title: Define NornError with domain-specific variants
requirements.spec: error.rs SHALL define a `NornError` enum using thiserror with variants covering every error domain in the design. Each variant SHALL carry a domain-specific inner error type also defined in error.rs. The enum SHALL NOT use anyhow, Box<dyn Error>, or String-only variants.
acceptance: NornError has variants: Provider(ProviderError), Schema(SchemaError), Tool(ToolError), Rules(RulesError), Agent(AgentError), Session(SessionError), Integration(IntegrationError), Config(ConfigError)
Each variant wraps a dedicated error enum defined in the same error.rs file
All variants have `#[error(...)]` attributes with descriptive messages via thiserror
`NornError` implements `std::error::Error` via thiserror derive
No `anyhow` import in error.rs
No `.unwrap()` or `.expect()` in error.rs

requirements.title: Define ProviderError and SchemaError
requirements.spec: ProviderError and SchemaError SHALL be defined in error.rs. ProviderError covers provider-level failures. SchemaError covers schema enforcement failures including the SchemaUnreachable terminal path. Both SHALL use thiserror. Both SHALL be re-exported from their respective module's mod.rs via `pub use crate::error::ProviderError` and `pub use crate::error::SchemaError`.
acceptance: ProviderError defined in crates/norn/src/error.rs with variants: ConnectionFailed, AuthenticationFailed, RateLimited { retry_after: Option<std::time::Duration> }, StreamError, ResponseParseError, UnsupportedFeature

SchemaError defined in crates/norn/src/error.rs with variants: ValidationFailed { schema: serde_json::Value, output: serde_json::Value, errors: Vec<String> }, Unreachable { best_attempt: Option<serde_json::Value>, validation_errors: Vec<String>, attempts: u32 }, InvalidSchema { reason: String }
provider/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::ProviderError;`
loop/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::SchemaError;`
Both types use thiserror derive

requirements.title: Define ToolError, AgentError, SessionError, RulesError, IntegrationError, ConfigError
requirements.spec: Each remaining error domain SHALL have a dedicated error enum defined in error.rs with variants reflecting the design's error scenarios. All SHALL use thiserror. Each module SHALL re-export its relevant error type from mod.rs via `pub use crate::error::XxxError`.
acceptance: ToolError defined in error.rs with variants: PreValidationFailed, ExecutionFailed, PostValidationFailed, ToolNotFound
AgentError defined in error.rs with variants: SpawnFailed, NotFound, MailboxClosed, PathInvalid
SessionError defined in error.rs with variants: EventAppendFailed, InvalidEventId, StorageError
RulesError defined in error.rs with variants: ParseFailed, TriggerEvalError, DeliveryFailed
IntegrationError defined in error.rs with variants: ClaudeRunnerError, McpError, RhaiError, HookError
ConfigError defined in error.rs with variants: InvalidConfig, MissingField
tool/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::ToolError;`
agent/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::AgentError;`
session/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::SessionError;`
rules/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::RulesError;`
integration/mod.rs contains `pub use crate::error::IntegrationError;`
cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors

requirements.title: Add serde_json dependency
requirements.spec: Cargo.toml SHALL add serde and serde_json as workspace dependencies. These are required for SchemaError's Value fields and will be used throughout the crate. serde's derive feature SHALL be enabled.
acceptance: Cargo.toml [dependencies] includes serde = { workspace = true, features = ["derive"] }
Cargo.toml [dependencies] includes serde_json = { workspace = true }
cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors

requirements.title: Validate mod.rs discipline across all modules
requirements.spec: This is a verification-only requirement — it validates a constraint without modifying files. Every mod.rs file in the crate SHALL contain only `pub mod` declarations, `pub use` re-exports, and module-level documentation. mod.rs SHALL NOT contain functions, traits, struct definitions, enum definitions, or implementations. Error enums are defined in error.rs and re-exported via pub use in mod.rs, which satisfies this constraint.
acceptance: grep -rn 'fn \|struct \|enum \|trait \|impl ' crates/norn/src/*/mod.rs returns no matches (pub use re-exports of error enums are not definitions)
Every mod.rs file contains only pub mod, pub use, and doc comments

boundaries: SHALL NOT define any provider implementations — only the ProviderError type
SHALL NOT define the Tool trait or any tool implementations — only the ToolError type
SHALL NOT define the Provider trait — only the ProviderError type
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
SHALL NOT modify the module directory structure — only fill in existing scaffold files
SHALL NOT define error types outside error.rs — all error enums live in error.rs, modules re-export via pub use
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
Every NornError variant wraps a dedicated error enum, not String
All domain error types are defined in error.rs
Each module re-exports its error type via pub use crate::error::XxxError
Every mod.rs contains only pub mod/pub use — no logic
